label_values.label: Caption
label_values.value:
I just want to share my day today. 

We were doing a community outreach sa mga kinder garten, giving school supplies, snacks, and some burgers. Then out of nowhere I checked my wallpaper(settings) and saw a picture of me and bobet. And ang funny part is naga teary eyes ako while doing an outreach hahaha.

Gigawa ko tong mga pag outreach because so many blessings came not just to me, but to our whole DAVRAA team. And itâs all because of Bobet. Bait mo talaga bet hahaha. So I just want to share the blessings na binigay din ni Bobet and ni Lord sa amin. 

Btw, this picture was taken in 2026 DAVRAA meet. Pag dating namin sa place na stayhan namin, sabi nya randomly âpicture ta ron(Aaron)â sa tanan sa amo, sakoa  gyud sya nakig picture og kami rapud nag picture hahahaha. Yah, thatâs the story behind this picture. 

I love you and I miss you, bet.

label_values.label: Caption
label_values.value:
Healthy posture is supported by how well your body movesânot by trying to keep your shoulders or spine in a fixed position all day.

Limited movement, prolonged sitting, and repetitive daily habits can contribute to stiffness in the neck, shoulders, and upper back. Regular mobility and strengthening exercises may help keep these areas moving comfortably.

The exercises in this routine include movements designed to:
âï¸ Improve mobility in the neck, shoulders, and thoracic spine
âï¸ Increase flexibility and range of motion through the upper body
âï¸ Strengthen the muscles that support posture and spinal stability
âï¸ Reduce feelings of tightness associated with prolonged sitting
âï¸ Support smoother, more efficient movement during everyday activities

Perform each exercise with slow, controlled movements and avoid pushing into pain. Gradually building strength and mobility together can help improve movement quality and support long-term spinal health.

If symptoms persist, worsen, or are accompanied by numbness, tingling, or dizziness, seek advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

â ï¸ Educational purposes only. Not medical advice.

via @drrodpt
